verdict_reasoning: TGFB1 is secreted but exists in a large latent complex (LLC) where LAP and the mature dimer are non-covalently and disulfide-tethered to LTBP proteins, which in turn are covalently cross-linked to extracellular matrix or can be tethered to cell-surface integrin/receptor scaffolds (e.g., LRRC32/GARP, LRRC33/NRROS) via disulfide bonds — a wash-resistant, stable cell-surface association on immune cells and other cell types. This is a documented stable_surface_attachment mechanism rather than purely secreted-only, qualifying as contextual surface accessibility.
